political.s: Regeneron shows right-leaning political leanings with $191K in FEC donations tilted toward Republicans (16,000 of 23,500 in donations). The company has engaged in lobbying and government partnerships, including a $450M government contract for COVID-19 treatment development and a 2025 pricing agreement with the Trump administration.

charity.s: Regeneron demonstrates strong charitable commitment with over $2.5 billion donated to independent patient assistance organizations over the past decade. The company established a $200 million donation matching program in 2025 and supports extensive STEM education initiatives including the Regeneron Science Talent Search and International Science & Engineering Fair.
charity.focus: Patient access, STEM education, disease prevention

environment.s: Regeneron has demonstrated environmental commitment through sustainability practices and achieved inclusion on the Dow Jones Sustainability World Index for seven consecutive years. The company reduced Scope 1 & 2 emissions by 11% since 2021 and responds to CDP Climate Change disclosures annually.

labor.s: Limited publicly available OSHA violation data found for Regeneron. The company emphasizes compliance with occupational safety standards and has established comprehensive EHS programs. Company culture appears positive with recognition in Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For, though wage equity metrics show significant CEO-to-median employee pay disparity.

dei.s: Regeneron demonstrates strong pro-DEI stance with a dedicated Chief Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Officer appointed in 2021. The company committed $3.1M+ annually to STEM outreach for underrepresented groups, launched DEI research grants, and established comprehensive diversity initiatives. Strategy focuses on 'Better Workplace, Better Science, Better World.'

animals.s: Regeneron conducts animal testing as part of pharmaceutical development and holds AAALAC International accreditation. The company follows the 3Rs principles (Replace, Reduce, Refine) and maintains institutional animal care standards, though it is listed on Cruelty Free Investing's animal use list due to reliance on animal testing.

privacy.s: Regeneron displays mixed privacy practices. The company commits to responsible data management in clinical research and professional communications, though specific third-party privacy certifications or detailed transparency practices are not prominently documented.

execPay.s: Regeneron's CEO Leonard Schleifer earned $6.82M in 2024 compensation, representing a 41:1 CEO-to-median employee pay ratio according to AFL-CIO data. While historical pay packages have been substantial (exceeding $100M in prior years), current compensation is below industry median for comparable biotechnology firms at $17M.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
